title: Mutton Rogan Josh
description: Rogan Josh is a signature dish of Kashmiri cuisine, celebrated for its deep red hue, aromatic spices, and rich flavor. Traditionally slow-cooked, it balances tender mutton with layers of spices and yogurt for a velvety, luxurious curry.

ingredients.title: For the Mutton
items: Mutton (goat/lamb, bone-in preferred) – 1 kg, medium-sized pieces
Mustard oil – 120 ml (or ghee for a richer restaurant version)

ingredients.title: For the Spice Base
items: Kashmiri red chili powder – 3 tbsp (for color and mild heat)
Garam masala powder – 1 tsp (freshly ground for best results)

ingredients.title: For Creaminess & Balance
items: Onion paste – 2 medium onions, browned and blended (optional, restaurant versions sometimes skip this for purity)

ingredients.title: For Finishing
items: Warm water or mutton stock – 600–700 ml
Fresh coriander leaves – small handful (for garnish)
Saffron strands – 8–10, soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional, royal restaurant touch)

instruction.title: Step 1 – Preparing the Mutton
steps: Wash and pat dry the mutton pieces.
Heat mustard oil until it reaches smoking point; reduce heat.
Fry mutton pieces in batches until browned and sealed. Set aside.

instruction.title: Step 2 – Spice Infusion
steps: In the same oil, add whole spices (cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaves). Let them release aroma.
Add hing, then quickly stir in chili powder dissolved in 2 tbsp warm water (prevents burning).

instruction.title: Step 3 – Building the Base
steps: Return browned mutton to the pan. Toss to coat in spiced oil.
Add fennel powder, dry ginger powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt.
Lower heat and add whisked yogurt gradually, stirring constantly to prevent splitting.
(Optional restaurant touch: stir in onion paste here for a silkier gravy.)

instruction.title: Step 4 – Slow Cooking
steps: Pour in hot water or mutton stock. Cover and simmer on low heat for 60–90 minutes until mutton is tender. Stir occasionally.
Adjust consistency: gravy should be medium-thick, coating the mutton pieces luxuriously.

instruction.title: Step 5 – Finishing Touch
steps: Stir in garam masala and saffron milk (if using). Simmer for 5 minutes.
Rest the curry for 15 minutes before serving to allow flavors to mature.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: In restaurants, it’s often paired with naan, sheermal, or saffron pulao.
Garnish lightly with coriander leaves; avoid heavy cream to keep authenticity.

chefTips: Use bone-in mutton – bones enrich the curry with depth.
Do not rush the simmering – slow cooking extracts flavor and tenderizes meat naturally.
Balance color & flavor – Kashmiri chili gives rich red color without too much heat.
Oil separation is key – the dish is ready when the oil floats on top of the gravy.
